data.body: That may be true, but I'd argue that 'noir' games that don't do this are using noir as an aesthetic more than a narrative framing device. Remedy is very consciously channeling an existing archetype and the narrative trappings of the genre too. And they're very good at doing it!

data.body:
Unbeatable is an interesting rhythm game, in that I haven’t quite played one like it. The inputs are simple enough, but varying the direction that the “notes” travel gives my brain pause. 

I’m only a couple hours in, but the world they’ve built is neat, and the character design is fun.
#unbeatable
